t_ch: 行，我去回绝陶德吧。
t_en: Alright. I'll go and decline Todd's offer.

t_ch: 这些医典并不是什么绝密文献，也不记载什么禁忌内容。
t_en: These medical textbooks are not highly confidential files, nor do they contain forbidden knowledge.

t_ch: 可规矩不是由我来定。丹鼎司大乱后，所有的往来申请都要被复核了。
t_en: But I'm not the one who made the rules. After what happened in the Alchemy Commission, every exchange request has to be re-approved.

t_ch: 我也是最近刚结束外勤差事回到这儿，丹鼎司里经历了刀兵人患，到处乱糟糟的，许多书都不在书架上。
t_en: I just came back from a foreign business trip myself. The Alchemy Commission had experienced a huge upheaval and armed conflict. Everything is a mess. So many books have been displaced from the bookshelves.

t_ch: 我<color=#dbc291ff>甚至看到有几册书不知为何挂在了树上</color>。
t_en: I even saw some books <color=#dbc291ff>hanging off trees</color>.

t_ch: 玉络冲你笑了笑。
t_en: Yuluo smiles at you.

t_ch: 我还得继续清点库存，没什么事的话回头再聊吧。
t_en: I still need to continue making inventory. If there is nothing else, let's talk another time.

t_ch: （这里不像有书的样子…不过玉络说要<color=#dbc291ff>许多书都不在书架上</color>…那就在附近找找看好了。）
t_en: (There doesn't seem to be any books here... But Yuluo said <color=#dbc291ff>many books have been displaced from the bookshelves</color>... Let me look around.)

t_ch: （这里杂物颇多，不过玉络说要<color=#dbc291ff>许多书都不在书架上</color>，恐怕得翻箱倒柜地找找了。）
t_en: (There are lots of things here, but Yuluo said <color=#dbc291ff>many books have been displaced from the bookshelves</color>. I might have to dig through everything to find the books.)

t_ch: 从夹缝处不明显的油渍来看，这里或许存放过食物，又或者是新鲜出土的药材。
t_en: From the minute traces of oil stains at the creases, you think this place might have been used to store food or freshly-dug medical ingredients.

t_ch: 将书藏在这里属实是糟蹋了，但还好没有，你不禁松了口气。
t_en: It is a positive disaster to keep books here. Thankfully, the books were not damaged.

t_ch: 这里并没有《要药分剂》，还是到别处转转吧。
t_en: The Compendium of Remedies isn't here. I should check somewhere else.

t_ch: 箱子里空空如也，比化外民游客们的荷包还干净——会误信星槎海「百年老店今日店庆」促销广告的那种。
t_en: The boxes are more empty than the wallets of the outworlder tourists — the kind of tourists who would believe the ads about "a centuries-old establishment having an anniversary sale" in Starskiff Haven.

t_ch: 这里并没有《要药分剂》，还是到别处转转吧。
t_en: The Compendium of Remedies isn't here. I should check somewhere else.

t_ch: 只听过「瓮中捉鳖」的，倒没听说过瓮中捉「书」，但这种稀奇事儿竟被你碰上了！
t_en: People talk about shooting fish in a barrel, but never about getting books out of one. You're lucky to encounter such a rare occurrence!

t_ch: 今天的快乐是书给的，你找到了《要药分剂》其中一册。
t_en: Today, your happiness came from books. You found one of the volumes of the Compendium of Remedies.

t_ch: 也不知道能赔多少……
t_en: How much would the insurance payout be...

t_ch: 呃？你就是「鹤运速递」的赔付专员？不过看你这打扮…怎么感觉是个化外民，难道是我认错人了？
t_en: Um? Are you the claims specialist from Heron Express? But your clothes... Why do I feel like you're an outworlder? Did I get the wrong person?

t_ch: 什么赔付专员？
t_en: What's a claims specialist?

t_ch: 就是「鹤运速递」专门负责处理寄件事故和售后的。
t_en: They're Heron Express staff that specifically manage shipping incidents and provide after-sale support.

t_ch: 化外民怎么你了…
t_en: What did outworlders ever do to you?

t_ch: 我不是那个意思，我在等我们本地的「鹤运速递」的专员上门呢，结果认错人了，有点失望。
t_en: That's not what I mean. I'm waiting for the local Heron Express specialist to arrive. I'm just disappointed to see someone else.

t_ch: 云骑的人说这附近不大安全，让我先回家休息几天。但我医书还没背熟呢，我就花了大价钱找「鹤运速递」寄件，把书从丹鼎司托运回家。
t_en: The Cloud Knights said it's not safe around here and that I should stay at home for a few days, but I still haven't memorized my medical textbooks. So, I spent a hefty sum to have Heron Express deliver the books from the Alchemy Commission to my home.

t_ch: 结果那机巧鸟还没飞出丹鼎司，一路上不是撞到墙就是撞树上去了！气死我了，我就眼睁睁看着我的书从天上掉下来……

t_ch: 哎，我的医书啊！我宝贵的医书！「鹤运速递」不赔我个百八十万，这事我定和他们没完！
t_en: Oh, my textbooks! My precious textbooks! If Heron Express doesn't pay me a million or two, they'll never hear the end of it from me!

t_ch: （向他询问医典的位置）
t_en: (Ask him where the textbooks are)

t_ch: …你要找《要药分剂》？巧了，有一册恰好在我手上。
t_en: ... You're looking for the Compendium of Remedies? Lucky for you, I do have one of the volumes.

t_en: {NICKNAME}, old Todd can only rely on you now.

t_ch: 实话告诉你吧，这味药来自仙舟人堕入魔阴身后的增生产物。若不猎杀仙舟人，便不可能得到。

t_en: Have you really seen this kind of mushroom before?

t_ch: 噢，我真为你那独属于灵长目人科人属的偏见而感到悲伤。
t_en: Oh, I feel true pity for this bias of yours that's exclusive to primates belonging to the family hominid and genus homo.

t_ch: 是你张贴的寻物启事吗？
t_en: Did you put up the Lost and Found notices?

t_ch: …我确实张贴了很多寻物启事，但是你说的具体是哪一张呢？
t_en: ...I have indeed put up many Lost and Found notices. Which one specifically are you talking about?

t_ch: 噢，是它呀，忧郁的诗人。你找到了什么线索吗？
t_en: Oh, that one. The Gloomy Poet. Have you found any clues about it?

t_ch: 听你这语气，还有很多诗人？
t_en: When you put it like that, it sounds like there are multiple poets?

t_ch: 嗯，没错，除了「忧郁诗人」，我还做了好几个其他的类型的「诗人」。
t_en: Mmm, that's right. I made several other poets besides the gloomy one.

t_ch: …这、这不会都要找回来吧？
t_en: Y—you can't be serious about getting them all back, can you?

t_ch: 当然都要找回来了！
t_en: Of course I want them all back!

t_ch: 我知道「阴晴伞」在哪里…
t_en: I know where the Baroumbrella is...

t_ch: 太好了，没想到这么快就收到好消息了。
t_en: Excellent. I never thought I'd get such a speedy reply.

t_ch: 目前没有线索，但我可以帮你。
t_en: No clues at the moment — but I can help you out.

t_ch: 太好了，我一直在等着像你这样的好帮手！
t_en: That's great! I've always wanted a good helper just like you!

t_ch: 我听人说「忧思为诗骨」。只要诗人足够忧郁，就能写出最打动人心的诗句…我原本对这位「忧郁的诗人」寄予厚望。
t_en: They say, "Gloominess is the backbone of poetry." If a poet is melancholic enough, the most beautiful poetic verse will flow out... I had such high hopes for this Gloomy Poet.

t_ch: 我把它「忧郁」的情绪权重设置得很高，它见着别人衣服上掉颗扣子都得嚎上半天。
t_en: I set its Gloomy setting very high, and it ended up wailing for half a day at the sight of a buttonhole missing a button.

t_ch: 到最后它就像大部分文艺青年那样，还没来得及写出个把像样的作品，就迷失在喧嚣的城市里了…咳，我希望它不是为生活所迫找了份工，那样的话也太不「诗人」了。
t_en: In the end, just like all other artistic youths, it got lost in the hustle and bustle of city life before it ever got the chance to write a decent piece... Ahem, I hope circumstances haven't forced it to have to get a job. That wouldn't be very poetic, would it?

t_ch: 我遇到它时，正在伤春悲秋。
t_en: When we met, it was very sentimental.

t_ch: 看来它还没忘本，但是时候该让它回家了。
t_en: Sounds like it hasn't forgotten its roots... But it's time for it to come home.

t_ch: 它太忧郁了，完全没法沟通。
t_en: It's so depressed that it can't communicate at all.

t_ch: 那可不是，过度忧郁对人类是一种有害的病症，对机巧来说则是故障了。
t_en: That's not really the case here. While being overly depressed is a serious illness for humans, for an ingenium it counts as a malfunction.

t_ch: 这就是为什么仙舟明令禁止大部分机巧不得模仿人类的情绪表征。那东西对机器不但多余，而且有害。
t_en: That's why the Xianzhou expressly prohibits most ingenia from imitating human emotion. Emotions are not only superfluous to machines — they are dangerous.

t_ch: 唉，可我有需要它忧郁的理由，只好苦一苦这台机器了。
t_en: *sigh* I have my reasons for wanting it to be depressed, so I'm stuck bringing despair to this bot.

t_ch: 这么矫情，打一顿就好了。
t_en: Don't be so dramatic. Give it a whack and it'll be fine.

t_ch: 你要是打坏了，还得赔我材料钱和工本费。
t_en: If you break it, then you have to pay me for labor and materials.

t_ch: 来吧{F#小姐}{M#小哥}，帮我个忙：<color=#dbc291ff>打开它的后盖，找到它的玉兆中枢，给它狠狠修理一发。</color>
t_en: Come on {F#miss}{M#mister}, help me out. <color=#dbc291ff>Open up its back flap, find its jade abacus core, and give it a solid repair job</color>.

t_ch: 只要能降低「忧郁」情绪的权重，它多半应该是会跟你回来的。
t_en: As long as you can reduce the Gloomy setting, it'll probably come back with you.

t_ch: 小聪让我找你回去。
t_en: Cong sent me to get you.

t_ch: 主人让你找一朵蘑菇干什么？
t_en: Why did my master ask you to find a mushroom?

t_ch: 更何况是一朵失败的蘑菇。
t_en: Let alone a failed mushroom like me.

t_ch: 小聪想找你去写诗。
t_en: Cong wants you to write poems.

t_ch: 为什么找蘑菇写诗？
t_en: Why ask a mushroom to write poetry?

t_ch: 我根本不懂写诗，我只懂得悲伤。
t_en: I don't know how to write poetry. All I know is sadness.

t_ch: 你主人叫你回家，他想你了。
t_en: Your master asks you to go home. He misses you.

t_ch: 为什么想要一朵蘑菇回家？
t_en: Why would he want a mushroom to go back home?

t_ch: 家里长出了蘑菇，可真是件让人忧伤的事啊。
t_en: It's truly a doom-and-gloom thing to have mushrooms growing in a house.

t_ch: （直接打开它的后盖）
t_en: (Open up its back flap)

t_ch: 怎么，你不知道什么是蘑菇吗？
t_en: Huh, don't you know what mushrooms are?

t_ch: 蘑菇就是蘑菇。
t_en: Mushrooms are mushrooms.

t_ch: 你要怎样才肯回到小聪身边？
t_en: What would it take for you to go back to Cong?

t_ch: 你总是对蘑菇的要求这么高吗？
t_en: Do you always place such high expectations on mushrooms?

t_ch: …要不我直接把你抬走好了？
t_en: ...How about I just pick you up and carry you off?

t_ch: 你为什么要抬走蘑菇？
t_en: Why do you want to pick up a mushroom?

t_ch: 蘑菇只是想待在原地，独自悲伤。
t_en: This mushroom just wants to wallow here alone in its own shroomy misery.

t_ch: 我也是蘑菇啊。
t_en: I'm a mushroom too.

t_ch: 你是什么种类的蘑菇…我无法识别。不，你不是我的同族。
t_en: What type of mushroom are you... I can't tell. No, you're not the same species as me.

t_ch: 我是一朵孤独的蘑菇，噢，真教人悲伤。
t_en: I'm a lonely mushroom. Alas, how sad am I.

t_ch: （直接打开它的后盖）
t_en: (Open up its back flap)

t_ch: 噢。我好悲伤。
t_en: Oh, I'm so sad.

t_ch: （直接打开它的后盖）
t_en: (Open up its back flap)

t_ch: （别浪费时间了，开盖吧）
t_en: (Quit wasting time and open the flap)

t_ch: （拖着忧郁诗人走）
t_en: (Drag Gloomy Poet away)

t_ch: 对这种矫揉造作的家伙，无需多言，直接上手把它拽走就好。
t_en: There's no need for more words with a pretentious thing like this. Just drag it away.

t_ch: 别…拽…我…了。
t_en: Don't... drag... me... away...

t_ch: 求求你，打开我的后盖。给…我…个…痛…快…吧。
t_en: Please, open me up. Set... me... free...

t_ch: 你想起了小聪的建议，找准了忧郁诗人的后盖，试图将「忧郁」情绪权重调低。幸好，忧郁诗人也没有拒绝的意思。
t_en: You remember Cong's suggestion. You locate the back flap and try to reduce the Gloomy setting. Luckily, there is no resistance from Gloomy Poet.

t_ch: 请你对我温柔一些，不要太粗暴。
t_en: Please be gentle with me. Don't be too rough.

t_ch: 你就像造主般无情冷漠，打开我纯洁的躯壳，解剖我悲伤的芯……
t_en: You are as cold and heartless as the Creator, peeling open my untainted shell and dissecting my depressed heart...

t_ch: 噢。我好悲伤。
t_en: Oh, I'm so sad.

t_ch: 嗯？我好像并没有那么悲伤。
t_en: Mmm? I don't seem to be that sad at all.

t_ch: 唉，一切都是程序预设，一切都是烟云，一切都是代码的白日梦，无休无止。
t_en: Alas, everything is but a predetermined program, all smoke and mirrors. Everything is but a daydream of code... never-ending mirage...

t_ch: 我没有如期归去，那原本才是别离的意义。
t_en: I didn't go back as ordered, as therein lies the true meaning of separation.

t_ch: 可是主人不明白，只顾执意呼唤我。
t_en: But my master could not comprehend, and kept trying to beckon for me.

t_ch: 还真是个诗人，属现代派的…
t_en: What a poet — and a modernist, at that...

t_ch: 走吧，我带你回家。
t_en: Come on. Let me take you home.

t_ch: 「人道海水深，我是大海坑。海水尚有涯，不如吃豆芽。」
t_en: "Humans compare depths to the ocean, I am a tsunami in motion. Oh ocean, a mistress so fickle, but I would rather just eat a pickle."

t_ch: …我这几句写得也没有什么毛病吧？
t_en: ...There's nothing wrong with this new verse I just composed, right?

t_ch: 你的诗人回来了。
t_en: Your poet is back.

t_ch: 哟，这不是我的诗仙机巧吗？
t_en: Ah, isn't that my little poet ingenium?

t_ch: …你还是别写诗了。
t_en: ...You're not great at this poetry business.

t_ch: 可是，她就是喜欢诗人呀…算了，我还是先瞧瞧我的诗仙机巧吧……
t_en: But she only likes poets... Forget it. I'd better take a look at my poet ingenium first...

t_ch: 你可算回来了啊，我还以为你被悲悼伶人给带走了呢？
t_en: You're finally back. I thought you were taken away by the Mourning Actors?

t_ch: 这都是你干的好事，你启动我的那一刻就为我输入了「忧郁」的指令。你给了我名为伤感的神罚，你想过我的感受吗？
t_en: This is all your fault. The moment you booted me up, you entered a Gloomy command. You gave me this divine punishment called sadness. Did you ever stop to wonder how I felt?

t_ch: 我感觉自己生来就注定要被世界抛弃，我不断忏悔、自我放逐，只是为从忧郁中提炼一点诗意，缓和我的悲伤。
t_en: It feels like I was destined to be abandoned by this world. And I continued to repent and self-exile, just to extract another morsel of poetry to ease my sadness.

t_ch: 这都怪小聪，非要写什么诗。
t_en: This is all Cong's fault, forcing you to write poetry.

t_ch: 很难理解机器人的忧郁…
t_en: It's difficult to understand a robot's sadness...

t_ch: 你不必理解我，你无法理解我。就像花儿不能理解雨云为何垂泪，这世上没有真正的「感同身受」。
t_en: You don't have to understand me. You can't really understand me. Just as a flower cannot understand why the rain clouds cry. There is no such thing as true empathy in this world.

t_ch: 为什么主人这么执着于写诗呢？
t_en: Why is my master so obsessed with poetry?

t_ch: 至少你的忧郁转化成了诗句…
t_en: At least your melancholy can be transformed into verse...

t_ch: 哎，大多数时候，忧郁就只是忧郁，不能写成任何诗。
t_en: Ahh, melancholy is just melancholy most of the time. It doesn't make for any kind of poetry.

t_ch: 为什么主人这么执着于写诗呢？
t_en: Why is my master so obsessed with poetry?

t_ch: 咳，我写诗…是为了赢得一个女孩的芳心。
t_en: Well, I write poetry... to win the heart of a fair maiden.

t_ch: 为了「爱」，人类俗套借口中最俗套的一个。我的芯不能理解这个概念。
t_en: For Love: One of the tritest of all human triteness. My chip has no way of understanding this concept.

t_ch: 我有一个喜欢的女孩儿叫静斋，她拒绝了我的表白。听说，她深爱着一位诗人，那位诗人出了一本诗集，而我却憋不出半截诗句。
t_en: I have my eye on a girl named Jingzhai, but she shot me down. I heard that she was in love with a poet and that this poet has a whole book of poetry published — but me, I could barely put together a rhyming couplet.

t_ch: 我琢磨着，办法总比困难多不是？作为工造司的成员，我的双手虽然写不出什么佳句，但可以造出写情诗的「诗人」啊！
t_en: I thought to myself, there have to be more solutions than problems in this world, right? As a member of the Artisanship Commission, I may not be able to put together any kind of poetry myself — but these hands of mine could sculpt a different kind of poet, if you get my drift!

t_ch: 人都有对象了。
t_en: C'mon, she's already got a partner.

t_ch: 我已经悄悄打听过了，她的同事们说，她现在单身来着。
t_en: I may have been asking around. Her colleagues said that she's single right now.

t_ch: 想来那个诗人也是个薄幸渣男…不过也好，这不，我就有机会了？
t_en: Feels like that poet is not that great a lover after all. But that's alright, because otherwise I wouldn't be getting this chance, would I?

t_ch: 她拒绝的不是诗，是你。
t_en: It wasn't poetry she rejected — it was you.

t_ch: 真的吗？我不信。她明明说过，她过去的爱人是位妙笔生花的大诗人，她深深为对方的诗才所倾倒。
t_en: Really? I don't believe you. She told me before that her past lover was a talented poet, and how she was infatuated with his poetic talent.

t_ch: 我总得有个可以努力超越的目标吧？
t_en: I have to set myself a lofty goal to surpass, right?

t_ch: …这能算是你自己写出来的吗？
t_en: ...Does this really count as having written it yourself?

t_ch: 相关法律我查过啦，仙舟上机巧不享有财产所有权。所以这把伞写的诗，版权属于我，怎么不算是我写的？
t_en: I've checked the relevant legislation, and ingenia do not enjoy property rights aboard the Xianzhou. So all the copyrights for all the poems written by it belong to ME — of course it counts as my writing!

t_ch: 说起来，我造的诗仙机巧不止一台，目前还有<color=#dbc291ff>三个家伙</color>采风未归，不知道他们运行得如何了。
t_en: Speaking of which, I've built more than just one of these poet ingenia. And there're still <color=#dbc291ff>three of them</color> out there who haven't returned. I wonder if they're still running.

t_ch: 要是能把它们的灵感玉兆带回来，应该能写出足以打动静斋的情诗了。
t_en: If you could bring back their Inspo Jade Abaci, then I should be able to craft a poem that'll move my dear Jingzhai.

t_ch: 我说，「蘑菇」，你应该能定位到其他几个诗仙机巧的位置，对吧？
t_en: Mushroom, you should be able to pinpoint the locations of the other poet ingenia, right?

t_ch: 我是诗人，我不是什么导航软件。
t_en: I am a poet, not some piece of navigation software.

t_ch: 搜到了，「边塞诗人」跑到了鳞渊境洞天里。
t_en: Found one: The Borderland Poet is over in the Scalegorge Waterscape delve.

t_ch: 我出发了。
t_en: Heading over now.

t_ch: 你好主动啊，一定是我对爱的执着打动了你是吧？呃，也有可能是报酬……
t_en: That's so proactive. Was it my perseverance for love that moved you to action? Hmm, I guess it could be the promise of reward, too...

t_ch: 所以，要派活了是吧？
t_en: Now's the time you're going to ask me to work, right?

t_ch: 你好懂啊。这么熟练，你一定经常助人为乐吧？
t_en: You get it. You're so proficient. You must help people out for fun, right?

t_ch: 怎么还有边塞诗人？
t_en: How is there a Borderland Poet?

t_ch: 呃，我其实也不知道静斋那位爱人到底是什么流派的诗人，所以一口气造了好几个机巧，各个流派尽在我掌握！
t_en: Uh, I actually don't know what kind of poet Jingzhai's ex is, so I've rolled out a few different ones — so I can master each genre of poetry!

t_ch: 拜托你了，{NICKNAME}，找回这些机巧，您就是我的再生媒人啊！我不会亏待你的。
t_en: Please, {NICKNAME}, find these ingenia, and you'll be my celebrity matchmaker for real! I'll reward you well.

t_ch: 「万壑千岩水自流，云深何处觅蓬丘。回首骁骑征途路，鳞渊境里试身手。」
t_en: "Rugged rocks and gorges winding through, where clouds hide the peaks from view. A glance back at the journey true, in Waterscape Gorge, I test anew."

t_ch: 来者何人？竟敢从背后接近老夫！
t_en: Who goes there? Dare thou approach this old timer from behind!

t_ch: 老夫？
t_en: Old timer?

t_ch: 我深度学习了「边塞诗派」的代表作，提取了作者的生平经历与词频风格。发觉这些作者各个都饱经沧桑，阅历过人。
t_en: I studied in-depth all the classics of borderland poetry of yore, distilling each poet's life essence and style. Realizing that each poet had encountered many diverse and rich experiences.

t_ch: 我被启动才过了两个月又十二天零八个系统时，实在没法和他们相提并论，只能先把自称改了，好揣摩一下诗人的心境。
t_en: I've only been running for two months, twelve days, and eight system hours, and I'm no match for them — so I've changed my personal pronoun to try and fathom the minds of such poets.

t_ch: 好个「边塞诗人」！
t_en: How very Borderland Poet of you!

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈哈！看来老夫我已经诗名在外了。
t_en: Hahaha! Seems my fame has already preceded me.

t_ch: 我有一种要打架的预感。
t_en: I have a feeling that we're going to fight, aren't we?

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈哈！{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}多虑了，老夫没有理由对你出手。
t_en: Hohoho! You worry too much, {F#young lady}{M#young sir}, I've no reason to hurt you.

t_ch: 噢。你如此高大，我却贫弱又矮小。我好悲伤。
t_en: Oh, your figure so imposing and tall, while I'm feeble and short. How tragic.

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈哈！主人还在折腾那些软绵绵、酸溜溜的诗吗？
t_en: Hohohoho! Is the master still playing around with those soppy, limp poems?

t_ch: …我认为主人写的那些糟粕不能算是诗，甚至连说糟粕都不配……
t_en: ...I don't think the master's doggerel can be considered poetry. Even calling it "doggerel" is being generous...

t_ch: 在理，是老夫对主人过誉了。
t_en: Sensible. I do give the master too much credit.

t_ch: 总之，把「灵感玉兆」交出来吧。
t_en: Anyway, hand over your Inspo Jade Abacus.

t_ch: 主人让我们俩来找你拿走你「灵感玉兆」。
t_en: Master asked the two of us to come and take your Inspo Jade Abacus.

t_ch: 「宁为百夫长，胜作一书生」，做不做诗人本就不重要，老夫以为，上阵杀敌才是头等大事。
t_en: "Better be a lieutenant than a scholar." What's important isn't whether you're a poet. I think that heading to the battlefield and striking down foes is more important.

t_ch: 老夫千里迢迢赶来这鳞渊境想要一展身手，没料想，云骑军对药王秘传的镇压已经结束，老夫扑空了！
t_en: I've traveled distances to come to the Scalegorge Waterscape just to show off my skills, but never expected the Cloud Knights' suppression of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus to be over already! I've come in vain.

t_ch: 哀哉哀哉，未经战场金铁交鸣的洗礼，老夫创制的句子实不敢妄称「边塞诗」。这「灵感玉兆」没有价值啦！老夫要把它毁掉！
t_en: Alas, without being baptized in the flames and steel of war, I daren't call any poetry I write true "borderland poems." This Inspo Jade Abacus of mine is worthless! I shall destroy it!

t_ch: 战争结束了，你不必再战斗了。
t_en: The war is over. You don't have to fight anymore.

t_ch: 条件：「边塞诗」需要体验「战斗」。输出结果：你们打一架呗。
t_en: Condition: [Borderland Poet] needs to experience [Combat]. Output Result: You two fight each other.

t_ch: 你挑事儿对吧？！
t_en: Are you trying to make trouble!?

t_ch: 那我们就来打一场吧！
t_en: Then let's fight!

t_ch: 原来如此！好主意，让老夫和你共赴沙场，用炽热的战斗激情碰撞诗歌的灵感吧！
t_en: Great idea! Let me take the battlefield with you, smashing the inspiration of poetry headfirst into fierce fighting passion!

t_ch: 非得加点儿战斗内容是吧？！
t_en: You really want to fight it out!?

t_ch: 你明白了，我可没明白啊！
t_en: You get it better than me!

t_ch: 先说好，打坏了你可不赔啊！
t_en: Let's agree that if I break you, I won't have to pay for you!

t_ch: 「杀气蔚蒸作烟云，寒光闪闪阵刀行。笑看白刃染朱红，云骑从来岂顾勋。」
t_en: "The air is filled with killing intent, rising like mist and cloud. The cold light shines, blades in formation marching loud. With laughter, watching as white blades turn crimson. The Cloud Knights care not for glory, nor their victory."

t_ch: 老夫，工造司安保金人丙型，前来讨教！
t_en: Artisanship Commission Security Aurumaton Type-C, here to seek guidance!

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Hohoho!

t_ch: 功名只应刀尖取，金甲豪杰一丈夫！
t_en: Fame should be earned at the point of a sword, while glorious armor maketh a lord!

t_ch: 柳林琼浆饮千杯，冲打陷阵胡不归！
t_en: At the Nectar of Willow Woods drink ye deep, rush headlong into battle to meet that eternal sleep!

t_ch: 帝弓贯虹无悔意，不破妖寇终不还。
t_en: Reignbow's piercing rainbow, never regret. Destroyer of demons, never forget!

t_ch: 再来！再来！嚯哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Encore! Encore! Hohoho!

t_ch: …很好，很好。老夫的灵感玉兆你们拿走吧。
t_en: ...Alright, alright. Here you go, my Inspo Jade Abacus.

t_ch: 老夫这一生，无怨无悔……
t_en: I... I've got no complaints or regrets in this life...

t_ch: 壮士保重。
t_en: Take care, brave warrior.

t_ch: 保重。
t_en: Take care.

t_ch: 差不多得了，也没怎么你…
t_en: That's enough melodrama out of you. It's not like I destroyed you or anything...

t_ch: 老夫还得谢谢你手下留情了。
t_en: I still have to thank you for going easy on me.

t_ch: 哼，手下败将。
t_en: Hmph. A defeated opponent.

t_ch: 可恶。但败阵的耻辱也将成为激励诗作的灵感。
t_en: How despicable, but even the shame of defeat serves as good inspiration for another poem.

t_ch: …我定位到了下一位诗人，就在星槎海，走吧。
t_en: ...I've located the next poet over in Starskiff Haven. Let's go.

t_ch: 我知道两位的来意呱，但是请你们先回答我的问题——写诗最重要的是什么？
t_en: I know why you're here, croak. But before we address that, please answer my question — what about writing poetry is of the most importance?

t_ch: 写诗最重要的…难道不是忧郁吗？
t_en: The most important thing about poetry... Surely it is depression?

t_ch: 是题材和对象。
t_en: It's about subject and theme.

t_ch: 光有题材和对象，却无深邃的诗情和巧妙的设喻，终究不过是一段大白呱。
t_en: Subject and theme alone without profundity, sentimentality, or the ingenuity of metaphor — without all that, it's just boring.

t_ch: 是韵律与格式。
t_en: It's about rhythm and style.

t_ch: 空有韵律与格式而无病呻吟，只能写出一堆乱码呱。
t_en: Rhythm and style alone make for moronic wailing. Just a bunch of bad code, croak.

t_ch: 是情感和意涵。
t_en: It's about emotion and meaning.

t_ch: 没错！看的出来，你很了解诗歌呱！
t_en: That's right — I can see that you have a deep understanding of poetry, croak!

t_ch: 是作者！
t_en: It's about the poet!

t_ch: 呱…你说的对，作者的才能决定了一首诗的优劣，但我问是诗歌创作本身！
t_en: Croak...You're not wrong. The poet's talent dictates the quality of a poem. But I asked you about writing poetry itself!

t_ch: 听着，写诗最重要的就是捕获人人都能共鸣的朴素情感。而在所有朴素情感中，离愁和盼归，是人类最普遍的共通情绪。
t_en: Look, the most important thing about writing poetry is capturing those simple human emotions which resonate with everyone. And of all the simple human emotions, the pain of separation and the longing for eventual return is the most universal across all peoples.

t_ch: 但是，可悲可叹呱！我在这乘船码头上已经等待半天了，却没有酝酿到足够的情绪，创作出一首颂离别的诗。
t_en: But, alas! Croak! I've been waiting all day here on this jetty but haven't managed to brew up the requisite emotions to compose a poem of departures.

t_ch: 什么是离别的诗？
t_en: What do you mean by "poem of departures"?

t_ch: 「相见时难别亦难」，讲述离愁别绪的诗歌就是离别的诗。
t_en: "'Tis hard to meet — and hard to say goodbye"... A poem that describes human emotion when parting from others is called a poem of departures.

t_ch: 诗又不是航船，还能等来？
t_en: Poems aren't spaceships. Can it come just by waiting?

t_ch: 「缘起成羁旅，归乡亦客身」，要是在乘船码头都等不到，在其他地方就更等不到呱。
t_en: "From wanderlust to sojourns afar, we all come home a visitor." If it doesn't even arrive at the departure jetty, then you won't be able to find it anywhere, croak.

t_ch: 可恨如今这罗浮交通便利，通讯方便。人们虽然乘舟离别，却不用担心归期何时，既没有殷殷的叮嘱，也没有深深的情谊呱……
t_en: I curse the convenience of modern-day transport and communications aboard the Luofu. People depart yet don't have to worry about when they'll return. There's no earnest pleading, no heartfelt emotion, croak...

t_ch: 一想到连离别都如此轻松惬意，我就想生气跺脚——虽然我脚不能着地。
t_en: The thought of how easy and satisfied goodbyes have become makes me want to stomp my feet in rage — even though I can't actually reach the ground.

t_ch: 你只是没有发现离别的眼睛。
t_en: You just don't have the mindset that can see goodbyes.

t_ch: 呱，这么说来，你能发现咯。
t_en: When you put it like that... can you, croak?

t_ch: 你能不能将就一下，随便写写？
t_en: Can't you just make do and write whatever?

t_ch: 不行呱！诗是不能将就的。
t_en: No way, croak! Poetry shan't ever just "make do."

t_ch: 你读过小聪主人写的糟粕吗？毫无感情，简直比杀了我还痛苦。
t_en: Have you read the doggerel that Master Cong writes? No emotion. You may as well just kill me.

t_ch: 在这里干等着也无济于事，你们帮我找找吧，这附近一定存在着<color=#dbc291ff>「感人的离别情景」</color>。
t_en: It's hopeless just waiting here. Please help me find what I'm looking for. There has to be an <color=#dbc291ff>emotional parting scene</color> nearby.

t_ch: 比如<color=#dbc291ff>「告别家人的远行者」、「依依不舍的恋人」、「各奔东西的友人」之类的情景。</color>如果你找到了，<color=#dbc291ff>请帮我拍成照片。</color>
t_en: You know the tropes, <color=#dbc291ff>"the voyager saying farewell to his family," "the star-crossed lovers bidding goodbye," "parting friends"</color>... that kind of thing. If you see anything, <color=#dbc291ff>please help me snap a photo.</color>

t_ch: 行，我帮你找找「离别情景」。
t_en: Alright, I'll help you snap a pic of a "parting scene."

t_ch: 拜托你了呱。
t_en: Thank you, croak.

t_ch: 我想转身就走，给你点离愁。
t_en: I want to bid you farewell right now to gift you an emotional departure.

t_ch: 不行啊，我对你没什么感情呱，当然也写不出诗来呱！
t_en: No. I don't have any emotions for you, croak. That's not enough material for a poem, croak!

t_ch: 你放心，只要我见到这些离愁别绪，诗作就能完成，我会把灵感玉兆交给你们，好让你们交差呱！
t_en: Don't worry. I just need to see that human emotion of parting, and the poem would write itself. Then I can give you my Inspo Jade Abacus, and you'll be done with your mission.

t_ch: 我今年六十二岁了，爸爸。
t_en: I'm sixty-two years old, Dad.

t_ch: 路上小心一些。
t_en: Be careful out there.

t_ch: 我今年六十二岁了，爸爸。朱明仙舟去过三次了。
t_en: I'm sixty-two years old, Dad. I've been aboard the Xianzhou Zhuming three times.

t_ch: 路上小心一些。
t_en: Be careful out there.

t_ch: 爸爸，你回去吧。
t_en: Dad, go home.

t_ch: 我买几个橘子去。你就在此地不要走动。
t_en: I'm going to buy some oranges. You stay put and don't go anywhere.

t_ch: 唉，我真想带你走！
t_en: Oh, I really want to take you with me!

t_ch: 那你为何抛下我？
t_en: Then why did you leave me behind?

t_ch: 唉，我真想带你走！
t_en: Oh, I really want to take you with me!

t_ch: 那你为何抛下我？
t_en: Then why did you leave me behind?

t_ch: 你家人都说我不思进取不顾事业。
t_en: Your family all said that I'm not a go-getter and I'm not career-focused.

t_ch: 呵，奋斗是吧？好借口。你以前怎么不说要出去奋斗来着？
t_en: Oh, now you're trying to focus on your career? Nice excuse. Why haven't you tried to focus on your career sooner?

t_ch: 是谁当时冲着我耳朵说些肉麻的情话，什么「重华，我爱你，直到魔阴降临」。
t_en: Who was it who once whispered something cringy romantic lines into my ears like "Chonghua, I'll love you forever until the mara comes."

t_ch: 你提这茬干啥，当时年轻…眼看咱俩好了快两百年了，可我还是连罗浮的房子也买不起。我总不能让你一辈子跟我挤在租屋里吧？
t_en: Why are you bringing that up? We were young back then... We've been together for nearly two hundred years now, but I still can't afford to buy a house in the Luofu. I can't just let you live in a cramped rental apartment with me for the rest of our lives, can I?

t_ch: 你别走，我可以和你一起还贷…
t_en: Don't go. I can help repay the mortgage...

t_ch: 不知先生是否满意？
t_en: Are you satisfied, sir?

t_ch: 我欲乘风归去…
t_en: Take me to my place of resting...

t_ch: 听说先生喜好远游。不知道此行是否心满意足？
t_en: I heard that you are an enthusiast for distant sojourns, sir. Are you satisfied with this trip?

t_ch: 我本短生种，以有涯之身而能造访仙宫，余愿足矣。
t_en: I'm a short-life species. To visit the Celestial Hall during my days is more than enough to sate me.

t_ch: 先生夸张了，这儿不过又一处人间罢了。
t_en: You're too kind, sir. It's just like any human world here.

t_ch: 此去一别，恐再无相会之日。届时我身归尘，还劳您远渡我故里，将骨灰带回仙舟，登临高处，洒将出去。
t_en: Let us say our goodbyes, then, for I fear we ne'er shall meet again. When the time comes, I'll return to the dust, whence I'll task you with a traveling quest. Please bring my ashes back to the Xianzhou, and spread far and yonder wherever you may go.

t_ch: 咳，这…先生的想法虽然极尽浪漫，只恐《罗浮治安管理条例》不肯。
t_en: Ahem, that's... Sir, that's an extremely romantic idea you have there, but I'm just worried that the Luofu Public Security Management Regulations will not agree.

t_ch: 我欲乘风归去，旅途轻快惬意，哗哈哈哈……
t_en: Take me to my place of resting, carefree travels interesting... Whoo-haha!

t_ch: 你给我回来！
t_en: Come back here!

t_ch: 妈妈，你别再劝我了。
t_en: Mom, I won't change my mind.

t_ch: 你给我回来！
t_en: Come back here!

t_ch: 妈妈，你别再劝我了。我已经买好了去曜青仙舟的船票。我一定要投效青丘军，建功立业！就像曜青那位将军大人一样！
t_en: Mom, I won't change my mind. I've already got my ticket to the Xianzhou Yaoqing. I have to join up with the Verdant Knights and make my contribution! Just like the general of the Yaoqing!

t_ch: 你船票落家里了…
t_en: You left your ticket at home...

t_ch: 啧…我怕你伤心，原想着悄悄离开，没想到还是被发现了。
t_en: Tsk... I was planning on sneaking out so you wouldn't be sad about it, but you noticed it right away.

t_ch: 我不拦你，只想告诉你，你看看票上的时间。
t_en: I won't stop you. I just want to remind you to check the time on the ticket.

t_ch: 什么？船票上写着明天……
t_en: What? The ticket is for tomorrow...

t_ch: 回家！现在，马上！
t_en: Come home! Now!

t_ch: 罗浮仙舟，我来了…
t_en: Xianzhou Luofu, I'm coming...

t_ch: 你好，请别忘了行李。
t_en: Hello. Please don't forget your luggage.

t_ch: 你刚才是不是在拍照？把照片给我看看！现在，立刻，快！
t_en: Were you taking a photo just now? Show me the photo now! Hurry up!

t_ch: 照、照片我已经删掉了！
t_en: I—I've deleted the photo!

t_ch: 你刚拍完，这就删掉了？我不相信，你拿给我看看。
t_en: Already? I don't trust you. Let me take a look.

t_ch: 请、请看！
t_en: P—please go ahead!

t_ch: 我现在就删掉……
t_en: I'll delete it now...

t_ch: 慢着！我就看一眼，我不会害你的。
t_en: Hold on! Let me just have a glimpse. I won't hurt you.

t_ch: 我刚才在拍风景，没有拍你。
t_en: I was taking pictures of the scenery, not you.

t_ch: 你别以为我没有注意到，你拍到我了，现在就拿来给我看。
t_en: Don't think I didn't see you. I knew you took a picture with me in it. Show it to me now.

t_ch: 请、请看！
t_en: P—please go ahead!

t_ch: 我现在就删掉……
t_en: I'll delete it now...

t_ch: 慢着！我就看一眼，我不会害你的。
t_en: Hold on! Let me just have a glimpse. I won't hurt you.

t_ch: 喏，这就是我刚拍的照片。
t_en: Look, this is the photo I just took.

t_ch: 陌生的女子仔细确认了照片，最后放心地将手机又还给了你。
t_en: After scrutinizing the photo, the unknown lady returns your phone to you with relief.

t_ch: 拍得很好看，我放心了。不过你要是会用美颜的话，再帮我瘦下脸谢谢。
t_en: The photo looks very nice. If you're good at editing photos, please help make my face smaller. Thanks.

t_ch: …噢，对了，你在这里拍什么呢？
t_en: ...Oh, by the way, what were you taking pictures of?

t_ch: 我在拍仙舟美人图，有男有女。
t_en: I'm taking pictures of good-looking people on Xianzhou. All genders included.

t_ch: 哦哦哦，我人到罗浮，刚下星槎，可让你拍着了。
t_en: I see. I just arrived on the Luofu and got off the starskiff when you took my photo.

t_ch: 我在拍「感人的离别情景」。
t_en: I'm taking pictures of emotional parting scenes.

t_ch: 可惜，我这不是离别，我刚从曜青仙舟来到罗浮，你应该去曜青拍我。
t_en: It's a shame that I'm not leaving. I just came here from the Xianzhou Yaoqing. You should have taken my photo there.

t_ch: 走了走了，你记得帮我美颜后发给我啊！
t_en: I've got to go. Remember to edit my photo before sending it to me!

t_ch: 怎么样，有拍到什么「感人的离别情景」吗？
t_en: How did it go? Did you capture any emotional parting scene?

t_ch: （这是我拍到的离别情景）
t_en: (This is the emotional parting scene I captured.)

t_ch: 人生欢聚难，惟有别离多。看到你拍的照片，我的仿生眼不禁潸然润滑液下——因为我没有泪腺。
t_en: In life, there are more partings than gatherings. Lubricant is trickling down my bionic eyes, as I don't have a tear gland.

t_ch: 我还得再去找找。
t_en: I'll continue my search.

t_ch: 此情此景，不写一首诗，实在是浪费！谁赞成，谁反对？
t_en: I must write a poem dedicated to this breathtaking scenery! Yay or nay?

t_ch: 我反对，别浪费时间了。
t_en: Nay. Don't waste your time.

t_ch: 我赞成。
t_en: Yea.

t_ch: 我反对，这和说好的不一样。
t_en: Nay. This isn't what we've agreed on.

t_ch: 你要是继续反对的话，我是不会交出灵感玉兆的。
t_en: If you keep protesting, I won't hand over my Inspo Jade Abacus.

t_ch: 但我还是想要灵感玉兆。
t_en: But I want the Inspo Jade Abacus.

t_ch: 写诗就写诗，小事一桩。
t_en: Go ahead and write it. It's not a big deal.

t_ch: 很好，这首诗要讲的是哪一种离别呢？
t_en: Great. What kind of parting should this poem talk about?

t_ch: 「子女远行与父母分别」。
t_en: "The parting of children who travel far from their parents."

t_ch: 即便人事错迁，谋生艰难，但父母面对孩子即将远行，仍是关怀备至。拳拳依恋之情，几分惆怅，尽在隐忍与不言之中……
t_en: Even if life is full of misfortunes and hardships, Parents always care deeply when their children leave. Their love and attachment, mixed with a tinge of sorrow, Are all expressed in their silence and restraint...

t_ch: 「恋人之间的依依不舍」。
t_en: "The parting of a couple in love."

t_ch: 身不由己、前途多舛，相识相知的恋人即将分离，他们许下了难以实现的承诺，各自心中都清楚重逢遥遥无期……
t_en: Uncontrollable urge, future full of twists and turns, Lovers who know each other are about to part ways. Promises made but hard to keep, As both of them know in their heart that reunion is far away...

t_ch: 「至交友人各奔东西」。
t_en: "The parting of close friends."

t_ch: 知音难寻，至交好友即将远行。此一别山高水长，不禁忧心日后的寂寥。世事风霜，人生百态不过如此……
t_en: Rare is a true companion, a good friend about to depart. The separation is vast and the journey arduous, causing worry for the loneliness that will follow. The world is full of trials and tribulations, life's myriad ways are nothing more than this...

t_ch: 横霄飞桥跨浑云，行舟徙倚入画屏。临行千言作无言，父子______。
t_en: Flying over the skyborne bridge, I row my boat into a scene as beautiful as a painting. Silence replaces thousands of words at separation, for the father and his son...

t_ch: 何须话泪别
t_en: should not bid farewell in tears.

t_ch: 异地同惚恍
t_en: always will unite in thoughts.

t_ch: 二人皆社恐
t_en: are both introverts.

t_ch: …怎么还有点小聪主人的风格。
t_en: ...Why does that sound like Master Cong's style.

t_ch: 横霄飞桥跨浑云，行舟徙倚入画屏。临行千言作无言，父子{TEXTJOIN#46}。你确定要这样写吗？
t_en: Flying over the skyborne bridge, I row my boat into a scene as beautiful as a painting. Silence replaces thousands of words at separation, for the father and his son {TEXTJOIN#46}. Are you sure this is what you want to write?

t_ch: 没错，就是这样的。
t_en: Yes. That's it.

t_ch: 我再考虑一下。
t_en: Let me think about it.

t_ch: 我将这句诗刻在了灵感玉兆上，以此纪念今日之所见。来，给你。
t_en: I've engraved this poem on the Inspo Jade Abacus to commemorate the experience of today. Here you go.

t_ch: 楼宇空悬并蒂灯，回廊处处斗火层。恋人相逢难长久，______。
t_en: Paired lanterns hang in the building, and the corridors are competing in firelight brightness. A couple's reunion is often brief...

t_ch: 无言相望玉界门
t_en: as they silently gaze at the Jade Gate.

t_ch: 执手哽咽诉离愁
t_en: as they clasp hands in imminent sorrow.

t_ch: 说让我走我就走
t_en: if you want me to go, I'll go.

t_ch: …怎么还有点小聪主人的风格。
t_en: ...Why does that sound like Master Cong's style.

t_ch: 楼宇空悬并蒂灯，回廊处处斗火层。恋人相逢难长久，{TEXTJOIN#48}。你确定要这样写吗？
t_en: Paired lanterns hang in the building, and the corridors are competing in firelight brightness. A couple's reunion is often brief, {TEXTJOIN#48}. Are you sure this is what you want to write?

t_ch: 没错，就是这样的。
t_en: Yes. That's it.

t_ch: 我再考虑一下。
t_en: Let me think about it.

t_ch: 我将这句诗刻在了灵感玉兆上，以此纪念今日之所见。来，给你。
t_en: I've engraved this poem on the Inspo Jade Abacus to commemorate the experience of today. Here you go.

t_ch: 流离宾至浮槎落，彷徨仙临徂逝川。罗浮亲友如相问，______。
t_en: I travel on a skiff as a wandering stranger, drifting along as on a divine river. Should those who know me on the Luofu query my life...

t_ch: 一片冰心在玉壶
t_en: I'll say my heart is still as pure and righteous as jade.

t_ch: 星河重逢终有时
t_en: I'll say our paths shall reconvene among the stars.

t_ch: 吃了上顿没下顿
t_en: I'll say I'm living from hand to mouth.

t_ch: …怎么还有点小聪主人的风格。
t_en: ...Why does that sound like Master Cong's style.

t_ch: 流离宾至浮槎落，彷徨仙临徂逝川。罗浮亲友如相问，{TEXTJOIN#50}。你确定要这样写吗？
t_en: I travel on a raft as a wandering stranger, drifting along as on a divine river. Should those who know me on the Luofu query my life, {TEXTJOIN#50}. Are you sure this is what you want to write?

t_ch: 没错，就是这样的。
t_en: Yes. That's it.

t_ch: 我再考虑一下。
t_en: Let me think about it.

t_ch: 我将这句诗刻在了灵感玉兆上，以此纪念今日之所见。来，给你。
t_en: I've engraved this poem on the Inspo Jade Abacus to commemorate the experience of today. Here you go.

t_ch: 这下只剩下最后一只诗仙机巧了。
t_en: Now, there's only one poet ingenium left.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，快看，它在这儿。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, look! It's right here.

t_ch: 一定是小聪，派你来监工。任务太烦难，汝命恕难从！
t_en: Surely it's the wily Cong, sent you here to crack the whip. The task at hand, a tedious slog, my duty's weight hard to equip.

t_ch: …你感染了什么病毒？
t_en: ...What kind of virus did you catch?

t_ch: 人类，别以为我不知道你在变着法骂我，你以为我愿意这么五个字五个字说话吗？沟通效率太低了。
t_en: Human, I know you're criticizing me indirectly. Do you think I'm happy speaking in that rhythm all the time? It's so inefficient.

t_ch: 但谁叫咱被赋予的第一使命就是干这个。
t_en: I have no choice, as this is the primary task assigned to me.

t_ch: 你交出玉兆，各走各的道？
t_en: How about you hand over your Inspo Jade Abacus, and we go our separate ways?

t_ch: 好了，好了，没事学点好的。你以为我愿意这么五个字五个字说话吗？沟通效率太低了。
t_en: Hey, learn some manners when you've got downtime. Do you think I'm happy speaking in that rhythm all the time? It's so inefficient.

t_ch: 可惜我是个机巧，没得选。主人要我学习，我就得吞下海量的诗作。
t_en: As an ingenium, I have no choice. If my master wants me to study poetry, I must read of them in spades.

t_ch: 诗歌依托于语言的特殊韵律，只有符合韵律的排列组合才能叫作诗。小聪主人为了掌握诗歌的「规范」专门造出了我。
t_en: Poetry relies on the special rhythm of language. Only the permutations that conform to the rhythm can be considered a poem. Master Cong has created me to master the standard of poetry.

t_ch: 只要你在「韵律」、「对仗」上得到我的认可，我自然会把灵感玉兆交出来。不然你就别碍着我继续读诗学习。
t_en: As long as your rhythm and counterpoints meet my standard, I'll hand over my Inspo Jade Abacus. Otherwise, stop disturbing my poetry study.

t_ch: 哼，小意思。
t_en: Hmph, this is a piece of cake for me.

t_ch: 你这打油诗水平，也配考我？
t_en: How can you test me when you are not competent yourself?

t_ch: 闲话莫再讲，手底见真章。你到底接不接受，别妨碍我学习。
t_en: No more small talk, and show your true talent. Take it or leave it. Don't disturb my study.

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: 首先我要考察你的「韵律」。诗人会使同韵母的字在诗句中有规律地反复出现，从而造成一种声音回环的美——也就是押韵。
t_en: First, I'll test your rhythm. Poets often use words with the same rhyme regularly in a verse to create an artistic echo-like effect.

t_ch: 听好了，我的上一句是：「采菊饮酒黄昏后」。
t_en: Listen carefully. The first line is: "Picking chrysanthemums and drinking wine at sunset."

t_ch: 有暗香盈袖。
t_en: My sleeves leave a silhouette.

t_ch: 嗯，不错。
t_en: Not bad.

t_ch: 有鸡鸭鱼肉。
t_en: I have a good meal that is hard to forget.

t_ch: 我姑且算你押韵了吧。
t_en: I'll let you pass.

t_ch: 观玉枕纱厨。
t_en: I saw a jade pillow and a luxurious kitchen.

t_ch: 你没有押韵，我必须惩治你。但我念你还未把握情况，便再给你一次机会。
t_en: You did not rhyme with my line, and I must punish you for that. However, considering that you haven't got the swing of things, I'll give you another chance.

t_ch: 听帘卷西风。
t_en: I listen to the wind rolling up the curtains.

t_ch: 你没有押韵，我必须惩治你。但我念你还未把握情况，便再给你一次机会。
t_en: You did not rhyme with my line, and I must punish you for that. However, considering that you haven't got the swing of things, I'll give you another chance.

t_ch: 「天对地，雨对风。大陆对长空，山花对海树，赤日对苍穹。」
t_en: "The sky matches with the earth, the rain matches with the wind. The great land matches with the vast sky, the flower matches with the mountain, the red sun matches with the blue sky."

t_ch: 「对仗」，即要求严格的对偶。字数相等，结构相同，字音平仄相对，字义虚实对应…这可并不容易。
t_en: Counterpoint is about contrasting or combining two terms, phrases, or clauses with opposite meanings. This is not easy.

t_ch: 听好了，我的上一句是：「山中月影桃前槛」，平平仄仄平平仄。
t_en: Listen carefully. My line is, "Speech is silver."

t_ch: 岭腹松阴李后檐。
t_en: Silence is golden.

t_ch: 仄仄平平仄仄平。哼，你肯定是蒙的吧！不过，我认可了。
t_en: Hmph, that must be a lucky guess. Still, I approve of it.

t_ch: 湖上风光柳下亭。
t_en: Life is bitter or sweet.

t_ch: 平仄平平仄仄平。哼，没有对仗工整，我必须亲自将你赶走！
t_en: This clause does not contrast with my line. I must oust you myself!

t_ch: 阁下风流杨先生。
t_en: Mr. Yang is a flirt.

t_ch: 什么杨先生？平仄平平平平平。哼，没有对仗工整，我必须亲自将你赶走！
t_en: What Mr. Yang? This clause does not contrast with my line. I must oust you myself!

t_ch: 二四一三五六七。
t_en: Two is one plus one.

t_ch: 仄仄平平仄仄平…但是你完全没有对上字义，我还是必须亲自将你赶走！
t_en: This clause has no meaning at all. I must oust you myself!

t_ch: 也许是汲取了太多诗才，格律诗人在一阵尖锐的爆响后炸成了一团烟花。连一句「我一定会回来的！」都没来得及留下。
t_en: After an ear-splitting sound, the Metrical Poet exploded into a firework, probably from taking in too much poetic knowledge. It did not even say "I'll be back!" before it was gone.

t_ch: 你狠狠地教训了古板的格律诗人，你让它明白了自由派诗歌的力量——虽然它再也没有改变诗风的机会了。真是可喜可贺。
t_en: You taught the old-fashioned Metrical Poet a lesson and schooled it about the power of free verse. It was a shame that it no longer has the chance to change the style of its poems. How wonderful.

t_ch: 走吧，灵感玉兆和《流云集》都拿到了，我们可以去出发找主人了。
t_en: Let's go. Since we already have the Inspo Jade Abacus and the Drifting Clouds, we may set off to meet our master.

t_ch: 很好，我可以把灵感玉兆交给你。这本《流云集》我也交给你吧。
t_en: Great. I'll give you the Inspo Jade Abacus and this Drifting Clouds.

t_ch: 我不喜欢《流云集》，它太自由了，缺乏规制。但这确实是打动人心的好诗。
t_en: I'm not a fan of the Drifting Clouds as the verses do not follow a certain pattern. Still, I must say this is a good poem that stirs my heart.

t_ch: 走吧，我们去找小聪主人。
t_en: Let's go and find Master Cong.

t_ch: 灵感玉兆已经收集完成了。
t_en: We've collected all the Inspo Jade Abaci.

t_ch: 辛苦了，快给我吧。
t_en: Thanks for your hard work. Leave them to me.

t_ch: 「蘑菇」，接下来我要把这些玉兆里的符箓篆刻进你的脑瓜里，这样你就会写出「最美的情诗」！
t_en: "Mushroom," I'll engrave the talismans in these Inspo Jade Abaci into your brain so that you can write the "most beautiful love poem"!

t_ch: …我持怀疑的态度。
t_en: ...I have reservations about this.

t_ch: 应该是大功告成了，「蘑菇」快动笔试试。
t_en: It should be done now. "Mushroom," try writing something.

t_ch: …你给我几个关键词吧。
t_en: ...Give me some keywords.

t_ch: 小聪，喜欢，静斋…怎么样？
t_en: Cong, loves, Jingzhai... How about that?

t_ch: 你说了约等于没有说…静斋不是喜欢《流云集》吗，你想要这样的情诗吗？
t_en: That isn't helpful at all... Isn't Jingzhai a fan of the Drifting Clouds? Do you want a love poem like that?

t_ch: 啊，对呀，快给我看看《流云集》。
t_en: Oh, you're right. Let me take a look at the Drifting Clouds.
